message.content: The human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) causes acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS). HIV attacks and weakens the immune system, specifically targeting CD4 cells (also known as T cells), which are crucial for the immune response. Over time, as HIV destroys more CD4 cells, the body becomes less able to fight off infections and diseases. If left untreated, this can lead to the development of AIDS, the most severe phase of HIV infection, where the immune system is significantly compromised, making the individual vulnerable to opportunistic infections and certain cancers.
